Ad Text:
Job Profile Summary

The OCM Senior Analyst plays a key role in designing, leading, and delivering organisational change initiatives that enable leaders, teams, and organisations to successfully adopt changes across business processes, systems, technology, and operating models. Working as a trusted member of the Unisys Organisational Change Management (OCM) team, this role partners closely with OCM Managers, project leadership, and senior stakeholders to drive measurable adoption outcomes.

The Senior OCM Analyst brings strong hands‑on experience, sound judgment, and methodological discipline to change delivery—owning workstreams end‑to‑end, coaching junior team members, and ensuring change activities are aligned to business outcomes, risk management, and benefits realisation.

Key Responsibilities

Change Strategy & Delivery

Lead the design and execution of OCM activities aligned to project objectives, business outcomes, and stakeholder needs.

Conduct and interpret change impact, readiness, and risk assessments at individual, team, and organisational levels.

Develop and tailor integrated change strategies, including communications, engagement, training, and resistance management plans.

Proactively identify change risks and adoption barriers; design mitigation strategies to address resistance and capability gaps.

Track, analyse, and report on adoption metrics, behavioural indicators, and change effectiveness to inform decision‑making.

Contribute to sustainment planning to embed change into business‑as‑usual operations.

Translate complex transformation initiatives into clear, actionable messages for diverse audiences.

Communication & Engagement

Lead the development of high‑impact, audience‑specific communications (executive briefings, change narratives, FAQs, campaigns).

Create multimedia and digital collateral (videos, infographics, intranet content) to drive awareness and engagement.

Ensure messaging is consistent, timely, and aligned with overall program governance and brand standards.

Training & Learning Support

Lead training needs analysis and learning strategy development aligned to role‑based capability requirements.

Design and review training materials, learning journeys, and reinforcement mechanisms.

Support and co‑facilitate training sessions for frontline staff, leaders, and executives as required.

Ensure training effectiveness is measured and iterated based on feedback and adoption outcomes.

Collaboration & Project Support

Work autonomously while aligning closely with OCM Managers, Program Leads, and delivery partners.

Facilitate workshops, feedback sessions, and stakeholder briefings to gather insights and refine approaches.

Contribute to OCM practice development, standards, templates, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Support governance activities including status reporting, risk escalation, and executive updates.

Required Skills & Capabilities

Strong written, verbal, and visual communication skills in English; additional languages (e.g., German, Dutch, other European languages) are an advantage.

Proven ability to design and deliver end‑to‑end change management activities across complex initiatives.

Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills, including experience working with senior leaders.

Ability to synthesise complex information and data into clear insights and practical recommendations.

Solid working knowledge of change management methodologies (e.g., Prosci / ADKAR, CMBoK) and their practical application.

Creative mindset with interest in using design tools (e.g., Adobe Creative Cloud or similar) for basic graphic or video editing.

Proficiency with Microsoft Office tools (PowerPoint, Word, Excel, SharePoint).

High level of organisation, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously.

Proactive, adaptable, and comfortable operating in fast‑paced, matrixed environments.

Preferred / Desirable Skills

Experience supporting large‑scale digital transformation, system implementations, or operating model changes.

Background in Business Process Improvement, Knowledge Management, Learning & Development, or Instructional Design.

Familiarity with reporting and dashboard tools (e.g., Power BI, ServiceNow, SharePoint analytics).

Experience leveraging AI‑assisted tools (e.g., Copilot, ChatGPT) to enhance communications, analysis, or engagement.

Exposure to regulated, government, health, or enterprise environments.

Experience contributing to OCM practice maturity, tooling, or capability uplift initiatives.

Qualifications & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Communications, Business, Psychology, Organisational Development, Human Resources, or a related field.

2–5 years of relevant experience in Organisational Change Management, transformation, or related roles.

Prosci or equivalent change management certification (or progress toward certification) preferred.

Equivalent combination of education and experience will be considered.

We help the world run betterAt SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. 

Lead the Future of technology Architecture – Join Our Architecture Advisory team
Join a high-performing team at SAP where Architecture Advisors play a pivotal role in driving meaningful business transformation. In this role, you will act as a trusted partner to customers, providing architecture stewardship that bridges their business strategy with SAP’s solution capabilities to deliver measurable business outcomes. You will work closely with executives and stakeholders to shape transformation roadmaps, guide enterprise architecture decisions, and ensure that innovation translates into real business value. As part of a collaborative and forward-thinking team, you will help customers navigate complex technology landscapes, unlock new opportunities through SAP’s platform and AI innovations, and accelerate their journey to becoming intelligent, resilient enterprises. If you are passionate about combining strategic thinking, enterprise architecture, and customer impact, this is an opportunity to influence large-scale transformations while working alongside some of the most talented professionals in the industry.

About the Role:

As an SAP Architecture Advisor, you will play a strategic role in guiding customers through complex business and technology transformations. You will act as a trusted advisor to executive stakeholders, helping align customer business strategy with SAP’s solution capabilities to deliver measurable business outcomes. Through strong architecture stewardship, you will shape enterprise transformation roadmaps, ensuring that SAP solutions are positioned within a coherent and future-ready architecture. Working within a high-performance team, you will collaborate with sales, presales, and delivery experts to provide strategic guidance across the customer lifecycle—from vision and planning to value realization. This role requires both strategic thinking and hands-on architecture expertise to help customers modernize their technology landscapes, adopt cloud innovations, and unlock value from SAP’s platform, data, and AI capabilities. The Architecture Advisor is instrumental in building trusted relationships, influencing long-term transformation programs, and ensuring customers derive sustainable value from their SAP investments.

Key Responsibilities:

Serve as a trusted advisor to customers, connecting business strategy with SAP solution capabilities to deliver measurable outcomes.
Provide architecture stewardship across the customer’s SAP landscape, ensuring alignment with enterprise architecture principles and transformation goals.
Shape and articulate business and IT transformation roadmaps that leverage SAP’s cloud, platform, data, and AI innovations.
Identify capability gaps and recommend SAP-led solutions that enable innovation, efficiency, and growth.
Support customer engagements such as strategy workshops, architecture reviews, and transformation planning sessions.
Collaborate closely with Sales, Solution Experts, and Presales teams to shape solution strategies, develop Bills of Material (BoMs), phased transformation roadmaps, and solution architectures aligned to customer priorities.
Partner with Industry Advisors, Value Advisory, and Customer Success teams to connect industry insights, business value frameworks, and SAP capabilities into compelling customer transformation strategies.
Lead strategic architecture discussions and executive engagements with CIOs, enterprise architects, and business leaders.
Support the creation of customer-centric proposals and strategic deal shaping, ensuring architectural integrity and alignment with SAP’s long-term innovation roadmap.
Utilize LeanIX enterprise architecture tools to create and maintain key customer architectural artefacts, including capability maps, application landscapes, transformation roadmaps, and technology reference architectures.
Contribute to strategic customer engagements, including executive workshops, architecture reviews, and “Art of the Possible” sessions that help customers envision future-state capabilities.
Ensure strong alignment between business value, industry context, and technology architecture across customer transformation initiatives.
Ensure solution integrity and architectural consistency across complex customer environments.
Contribute to a high-performing advisory team by sharing best practices, industry insights, and thought leadership.

Experience

Proven experience in enterprise architecture, solution architecture, or strategic technology advisory roles within large enterprise environments.
Strong understanding of business transformation, cloud adoption, and enterprise IT modernization strategies.
Experience working with SAP solutions and platforms, with the ability to translate technology capabilities into tangible business value.
Demonstrated ability to engage and influence C-level executives and senior stakeholders.
Experience collaborating across sales, presales, and delivery organizations in complex enterprise engagements.
Implementation or program delivery experience in large-scale enterprise transformation initiatives is highly desirable.
Strong understanding of Public Sector or regulated industries is an added advantage. Flexibility to work in a mix of Industries is highly valued.
Domain expertise in at least one core business capability area, such as Finance, Spend Management, Asset Lifecycle Management, Supply Chain, or Customer Experience, is an advantage.
Excellent communication, storytelling, and stakeholder management skills with the ability to articulate complex architecture concepts in a business-relevant way.
Experience working with enterprise architecture frameworks and tools, including LeanIX, for developing architecture artefacts and transformation roadmaps.
TOGAF certification or equivalent enterprise architecture credentials are considered an added advantage.

Help design better digital services for young Queenslanders
Troocoo is partnering with a large Queensland Government agency to recruit a Principal Digital Research Officer to support a high-impact project improving how young people access support services.
This is an opportunity to apply human-centred design (HCD) and digital research expertise to create meaningful, user-focused online experiences that make a real difference.

About the role
You’ll join a specialist Strategic Communication and Engagement function delivering digital, communication and engagement services across the organisation.
Working within a small, agile project team, you will lead digital research and insight generation for a major initiative focused on developing resources for young people and their support networks.
The project includes:

Research and co-design with young people and stakeholders
Development of web content and digital resources using HCD methodologies
Supporting communication, engagement and campaign activities
Contributing to a targeted grassroots marketing approach
What you’ll be doing

Lead end-to-end human-centred design research projects
Plan and conduct user research, usability testing and information architecture validation
Generate insights to inform digital content, services and user experience improvements
Support reporting by identifying metrics and analysing performance data
Provide expert advice on digital best practice, accessibility and content standards
Contribute to publishing, editorial and governance processes
Stay current with emerging digital trends and technologies
Work collaboratively with project team members, stakeholders and subject matter experts
What we’re looking for
You’re a digitally savvy researcher who combines analytical thinking with a strong understanding of user needs.
You will bring:

Experience delivering HCD research and user testing for digital products or services
Strong knowledge of web content design, accessibility standards and best practice
Ability to translate research insights into actionable recommendations
Experience working in agile or fast-paced project environments
Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement and problem-solving skills
Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage competing priorities
Highly regarded:

Experience with tools such as Google Analytics or similar data platforms
Familiarity with CMS platforms (e.g. Squiz DXP)
Experience in government, community services or youth-focused projects
Why apply?

Work on a meaningful project with real social impact
Flexible work arrangements
Collaborative, multidisciplinary team environment
Opportunity to influence the design of government digital services
How to apply
Apply now via Seek or contact Neridah Day @ Troocoo – Neridah.day@troocoo.com / 0404 857 431 for a confidential discussion.

Creative Strategist (Paid Social / UGC)

Calmerceutical's is Australia's fastest growing supplement brand. We're in an incredibly exciting stage. We are a fast-growing, premium ingestible brand built on one thing: results. We're moving at the speed of light and looking for a unicorn creative strategist to join and become a pivotal part of the family.

We don’t follow industry norms. We don’t build products for margins. We don’t create content for vanity... Everything we do is designed to perform.

The role is flexible and can be fully remote.

About the role

We are looking for a Creative Strategist to lead the development of high-performing paid social content, taking it from good to great.

This is not a traditional “brand” or “aesthetic” role. This role is about performance and conversion - it sits at the intersection of creative and performance. Analysing data, trends, hooks (hooks and more hooks!) to really take creative assets to the next level.

Your job is to create content (not yourself behind the camera - but the concepts) that stops people scrolling, drives action, and improves conversion.

You will be responsible for ideating and iterating on creative across Meta and other paid channels, working with the team to create scroll stopping content.

What you’ll be doing

Developing hard-hitting, scroll-stopping concepts for paid social

Writing hooks that grab attention immediately

Building and testing multiple angles, messages and formats at speed

Analysing performance data to identify what’s working and what’s not

Iterating aggressively - cutting what doesn’t perform, doubling down on what does

Working with our content team to execute your vision

Continuously pushing for better, stronger, more effective creative

Key responsibilities

Develop high-performing creative concepts for paid social (UGC, founder-led, direct response)

Write hooks, scripts and angles designed to convert

Analyse creative performance and iterate based on data

Build and manage a structured testing pipeline (angles, hooks, formats)

Work closely with the performance team to align creative with campaign objectives

Brief and direct the team on content that you want to see

Review and refine content to maximise engagement and conversion

Stay ahead of trends while maintaining brand positioning

What success looks like

Proven experience creating high-performing paid social content (DTC preferred)

Strong instinct for:

Hooks

Messaging

Emotional pull

Conversion

You understand what makes someone stop scrolling and take action

You think in performance metrics, not opinions

You move fast, test fast, and improve fast

You’re not afraid to challenge ideas and push for better

Scroll-stopping creatives that drive strong engagement and conversions

Clear improvement in ad performance driven by creative output

Consistent pipeline of new angles and concepts being tested

Fast iteration cycles based on performance data

Strong alignment between creative and performance strategy

About you

Proven experience creating high-performing paid social content (DTC preferred)

Strong understanding of what makes content convert, not just look good

Skilled in writing hooks. We want a Hook unicorn.

Comfortable analysing performance data and adjusting creative accordingly

Fast-moving, proactive and highly organised

Strong attention to detail and brand consistency

This role is not suitable for candidates who

Focus primarily on aesthetics over performance. This is a data driven performance role above all else.

Prefer slow, highly polished creative processes - we move fast!

Are not comfortable or don't have experience working with performance data

To apply

Bonus points for:

Examples of ads or content you have created

A short explanation of your best-performing concept and why it worked

How you would approach creating content for a brand like Calmerceuticals

Who We Are

At Acusensus, we don’t just build technology; we change behaviours and save lives. We are an Australian technology company with operations across Australia, the United Kingdom, the United States, and New Zealand. Founded in 2018, Acusensus has built world-leading AI and sensing technology for road safety.

We’re now looking for a commercially minded AI Adoption Specialist to accelerate the integration of Artificial Intelligence across our global operations and embed AI as a practical, value‑creating capability across the business.

Why You’ll Love Working With Us
Mission with Meaning: Your work will directly support technology that saves lives and prevents injuries, applying AI where it delivers measurable real‑world impact.
Enterprise‑Wide Influence: This is not a lab-based or theoretical role, you’ll work side‑by‑side with leaders and teams to transform the way Acusensus operates through smart, practical AI adoption.
Innovation‑Driven Culture: Join a high‑performing, collaborative organisation that encourages curiosity, continuous improvement, and bold thinking while balancing innovation with responsibility.
The Opportunity

As the AI Adoption Specialist, you will play a pivotal role in advancing AI maturity across Acusensus. Acting as a bridge between strategy, operations, and technology, you will identify, prioritise, and lead high‑impact AI initiatives that demonstrably improve business performance, decision‑making, and operational efficiency.

Reporting to the Head of Product and Service Operations, this role is based in Sydney and works closely with senior leaders, delivery teams, and internal AI champions across the global business.

You will define and maintain a clear AI adoption roadmap, ensure initiatives are backed by strong cost–benefit analysis, and embed change through effective engagement, coaching, and change management.

What You’ll Do
Identify High‑Impact Opportunities: Evaluate business processes and workflows to pinpoint where AI can deliver the greatest strategic, operational, and financial benefit.
Link AI to Business Outcomes: Ensure every AI initiative is tied directly to measurable performance improvements and commercial value.
Lead AI Initiatives End‑to‑End: Take a hands‑on role in rolling out major AI‑driven initiatives, from concept through to full business adoption.
Build AI Capability: Develop and coach a network of internal “AI Champions” to drive localised improvements and sustained adoption.
Shape the AI Roadmap: Own and evolve Acusensus’ internal AI adoption roadmap, balancing innovation, risk, and return on investment.
Report and Advise: Provide clear reporting and insights to senior leaders on AI adoption progress, opportunities, risks, and emerging trends.
What We’re Looking For
Strong Technical & Business Foundation: Tertiary qualification in Information Technology, Data Science, Engineering, Business Administration, or a related field.
Proven AI or Digital Transformation Experience: Demonstrated experience implementing AI‑driven solutions or leading digital transformation initiatives in complex environments.
Commercial & Strategic Mindset: Ability to connect AI capabilities to tangible business outcomes using sound prioritisation and cost–benefit analysis.
Influential Communicator: Exceptional relationship‑building and influencing skills, with the confidence to drive change across all levels of the organisation.
Adaptable & Curious: A fast learner who thrives in rapidly evolving technology landscapes and enjoys exploring what’s possible.
What We Offer
Competitive salary package
Flexible working arrangements
National travel opportunities with global exposure
The chance to work with cutting‑edge AI and imaging technology
A collaborative, purpose‑driven global team
A culture built on Impact, Collaboration, Integrity, Care, and Delivery
How to Apply

If you’re passionate about applying AI in ways that truly matter and want to help shape the future of a fast‑growing global technology company, we’d love to hear from you. For confidential enquiries, please contact careers@acusensus.com.

Acusensus is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to make a meaningful impact

Most roles at this level start to look the same.

The platforms are there. The team is capable. The problems are known.

But your ability to influence how things actually operate is still limited.

You might own a function, but not how it connects. You can improve parts of the system, but not how data, engineering and architecture come together end to end. You can see what needs to change, but not always create the alignment to make it stick.

Over time, that starts to flatten things.

You’re solving problems, but not shaping outcomes.

This role is different.

It gives you the scope to step beyond individual domains and take ownership of how data, engineering and architecture come together as a whole.

Not just setting direction, but building alignment, lifting capability and making change land, while positioning the function for what comes next.

Why this role exists

The organisation has invested in its platforms and capability across data, engineering and integration. The core platforms are in place and actively used.

What hasn’t kept pace is how consistently it all operates.

Standards aren’t always aligned. Ownership isn’t always clear. Teams and partners are delivering, but not always in a way that builds momentum or scales.

Senior leaders are still closer to delivery than they should be.

This creates a gap between effort and outcome.

This role exists to close that gap, to bring clearer leadership across data, engineering and architecture, to align how teams work, not just what they deliver, and to establish standards that scale and create more predictable delivery.

The role

You will lead data, engineering and architecture across internal teams and partners. You will have clear ownership of how data, engineering and architecture operate together across the organisation.

This is not about being the most technical person in the room. It’s about how you lead, align and influence the room.

You will set direction and standards that teams can actually follow, bring structure to how decisions are made and executed, stay close enough to delivery to guide, challenge and unblock, work across business and technology stakeholders to align priorities, and ensure vendors and internal teams are working toward the same outcomes.

A key part of the role is how you lead. You will need to bring people on the journey, not just set direction, but build alignment. Not just move fast, but move in a way others can follow.

There is energy in the environment, but it needs to be channelled.

This role suits someone who can create momentum without creating noise, who can balance pace with structure, and who can challenge without losing people along the way.

You will also help shape how data and engineering evolve over time, including where emerging capabilities like AI can create meaningful impact.

You will operate with real autonomy, supported by an experienced CIO who values clear thinking, practical leadership and ongoing development.

Environment

A national business operating across manufacturing, retail and ecommerce.

It is a live trading environment where technology directly impacts performance across stores, supply chain and digital channels. Technology decisions have real, visible consequences, both operationally and commercially.

You will work across a mix of internal teams and external partners, where influence and alignment are as important as technical direction.

What you’ll bring

You’ve reached a point where your impact comes from how you lead and influence, not just what you know.

You’ve led teams, not just functions. You’ve improved how things operate, not just delivered outcomes. You’ve likely worked in environments where platforms were in place, but practices needed lifting.

You bring:

Strong leadership experience across data or engineering teams
The ability to bring stakeholders on the journey, not just set direction
Clear communication and influencing skills across business and technology
A solid grounding in modern data platforms, Fabric exposure beneficial
Genuine curiosity in AI and emerging capability, and a practical view on where it creates real value
The judgement to balance pace, structure and long-term outcomes
Experience improving delivery standards, not just delivering projects

You are comfortable engaging in detail when required, but you don’t get lost in it. You know how to guide teams, challenge constructively and create clarity where there is ambiguity.

This role will not suit someone who leads through policy alone, or stays removed from delivery. It suits someone who can engage, influence and elevate.

Next steps

For a confidential discussion, please contact Steven Fulop at xceltium on 0418 994 446. If this role aligns to where you want to take your career next, we’d welcome a conversation.

About the Team


Our ANZ Sales team drives DoorDash's growth across Australia and New Zealand. The team has built strong momentum through disciplined execution, structural simplification, and record-breaking performance across topline growth, merchant investment, and sales productivity. As we scale into our next phase, the strength of our strategy and operations function must grow with it.

About the Role


As our Head of Sales Strategy & Operations for ANZ, you'll own the strategy and execution engine behind our entire sales organization across all verticals and segments. This means designing go-to-market playbooks, building sales forecasting and incentive frameworks, optimizing merchant acquisition and investment motions, and driving cross-functional initiatives that improve how we sell, what we sell, and who we sell to. You'll be responsible for turning market intelligence and commercial insights into operating plans that drive sustainable, profitable growth.

We're looking for someone who is analytical, commercially sharp, and energized by building structure in a fast-moving, competitive market.

This is a hybrid role with 3 days a week in office and 2 days remote. 

You’re excited about this opportunity because you will…

Own the ANZ sales strategy across all verticals and segments, translating market dynamics, competitive intelligence, and merchant insights into actionable plans
Design commercial go-to-market motions including packaging, bundling, pricing strategy, and channel optimization across diverse business lines and merchant types
Build sales forecasting and incentive frameworks that align team behavior with strategic priorities and enable leadership to make informed investment decisions
Drive impact measurement across sales initiatives, building the analytical infrastructure to understand what's working, quantify incrementality, and scale what drives results
Build and lead a high-performing team, coaching and developing strategy and operations talent while fostering a culture of ownership, analytical rigor, and cross-functional partnership
Accelerate productivity through AI, identifying and deploying AI-powered workflows, rep enablement tools, and process automation that make the sales org faster and smarter
We’re excited about you because…

You have 10+ years of experience in sales strategy, sales operations, revenue operations, or management consulting, with a track record of building and executing go-to-market plans across multiple business lines
You're deeply analytical and comfortable building models to size opportunities, forecast growth, design incentive structures, and evaluate the economics of strategic initiatives
You have experience with AI-driven sales tooling and are excited about applying AI to transform sales motions, targeting, and rep productivity
You've operated in a challenger or high-growth environment and understand how to compete for share, manage complex commercial motions, and drive alignment across diverse stakeholders
You're a proven people leader with experience building, coaching, and scaling high-performing strategy and operations teams
You communicate with clarity and can influence senior leaders with data, narrative, and a strong point of view

About DoorDash


At DoorDash, our mission to empower local economies shapes how our team members move quickly, learn, and reiterate in order to make impactful decisions that display empathy for our range of users—from Dashers to merchant partners to consumers. We are a technology and logistics company that started by enabling door-to-door delivery, and we are looking for team members who can help us go from a company that is known as the place you order food to a company that people turn to for any and all goods.

DoorDash is growing rapidly and changing constantly, which gives our team members the opportunity to share their unique perspectives, solve new challenges, and own their careers. We're committed to supporting employees’ happiness, healthiness, and overall well-being by providing comprehensive benefits and perks.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion


We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.
